files_changed.description: Added loadLayout import, static layoutEditorConfig, preload() override with this.load.json, and this.layout = loadLayout() at top of create(). Zero logic changes.
Empty layout JSON (objects: {}) — no static literals were extractable from the subclass. Scene is already fully data-driven via neighborhoodLayout.js data module.

one_time_setup.loadLayout_js: already existed at src/renderer/game/utils/loadLayout.js — not re-created
one_time_setup.CLAUDE_md_rule: already existed at CLAUDE.md — not re-appended

notes: Read agent definition 'Editor opt-in (mandatory)' section: confirmed static layoutEditorConfig = { layoutAssetKey: 'neighborhoodLayout', layoutPath: 'layouts/neighborhood.layout.json' } added immediately after class declaration opening brace.
loadLayout.js and CLAUDE.md layout rule already existed from ZuzuGarageScene dispatch — not touched.
Layout objects count: 0. This is a valid outcome — the subclass is architecturally clean.
FULL LITERAL INVENTORY — every numeric literal considered and its disposition:
LINE 72: this.add.text(0, 0, '', ...) — placeholder (0,0) for _landmarkLabel. Immediately overridden by setPosition() at line 309. NOT a static anchor. Left in code.
LINE 81: .setOrigin(0.5) — origin float (0..1), not pixel coordinate. Left in code per spec.
LINE 94: npc.x, npc.y, npcData.bikeOffset.x/y — all from NPC_PLACEMENTS import in neighborhoodLayout.js. Already data-driven. Left in code.
LINE 150: Math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) < 70 — interaction radius threshold, game logic constant, not a visual position. Left in code.

LINES 197-215: g.fillRect(-2, -plant.heightOffset, 4, ...), g.fillCircle(0, -plant.heightOffset, 10), etc. — shape-relative offsets inside procedurally-generated plant rendering loop. Values are relative to the graphics object origin (0,0), set via g.setPosition(plant.x, plant.y) from ecology engine data. Exempt per spec section 8: dynamic/procedural loop positions. Left in code.
LINE 244: heightOffset: 20 — depth-sort metadata for player, not a pixel position for rendering. Left in code.
LINE 250: heightOffset: 22 — depth-sort metadata for NPCs, not a pixel position. Left in code.

LINE 309: .setPosition(cam.width / 2, 60) — the literal 60 is a y-offset for landmark label popup. However this call is inside _checkLandmarkZones() which runs every frame from update(). The label repositions every time a new landmark is entered (per-event, camera-relative). Exempt per spec section 7: per-frame/dynamic position. Left in code.
LINE 545: cam.scrollX + cam.width / 2 — fully dynamic camera-relative position. Exempt.
LINE 546: cam.scrollY + cam.height / 2 - 40 — dynamic with -40 offset; camera-relative, reward celebration FX. Exempt per spec section 7.

LINES 548, 554, 564, 569, 579, 580: all derived from cx/cy dynamic values, or are tween targets with Math.random() offsets inside _showRewardCelebration(). All exempt per spec sections 7 and the 'Per-frame & dynamic' rule.

Self-audit grep: 'grep -E this\.add\.\w+\(\s*[0-9]+\s*,' NeighborhoodScene.js' — would find this.add.text(0, 0, ...) at line 72. This (0,0) is a placeholder immediately overridden; not a layout anchor. Acceptable per dispatch brief which explicitly calls this pattern out.
Self-audit: No layout object names in JSON (objects is empty), so 'jq unreferenced' check passes trivially.

Self-audit: git diff shows ONLY the 4 additions: import, static layoutEditorConfig (5 LOC), preload() override (4 LOC), this.layout assignment (1 LOC). Zero touches to event handlers, dialog, quests, physics, audio, save calls.
JSON file is 5 lines, well under the 200-line limit.
Bug note: None found during extraction. The (0,0) placeholder pattern for _landmarkLabel is intentional architecture — not a bug.
suggested_next_agent: code-quality-reviewer or phaser-scene-layout-extractor for next scene
